en.title: For Starlink Performance Adapter Gen 3, For Starlink Performance Gen 3 Cable Extension to RJ45 Coupler Waterproof Compatible with Starlink Performance Gen 3 Dish
en.summary_html:
Plug-and-Play Connectivity:The Starlink Ethernet Adapter for Performance Gen 3 provides a seamless connection from your Starlink Performance Gen 3 dish to standard Ethernet cables. This plug-and-play solution eliminates the need for cutting or crimping the original Starlink cables, ensuring an easy and hassle-free setup.
Low Contact Resistance for Optimal Performance:This Performance Gen 3 Ethernet Cable features patented advanced soldering-type RJ45 connectors, designed to withstand all-weather conditions. These connectors reduce contact resistance by 50%, ensuring your Starlink setup stays in optimal condition for better performance and reliability.
Extend and Repair Your Network Setup:Effortlessly extend or repair your network setup with the Starlink Extension Repair Adapter, allowing you to connect to RJ45 Ethernet cables of your desired length. We recommend using cables under 164ft (50m) to maintain reliable operation, minimize interference, and avoid potential signal loss.
Dustproof and Waterproof Protection:The IP67 waterproof RJ45 coupler features a rugged Starlink cable kit that provides excellent protection against moisture, dirt, and other environmental factors. This ensures your network stays intact and performs consistently, whether indoors or outdoors.
High-Speed Data Transmission:Built with 23AWG 100% pure copper, the male-to-female extender ensures strong signal integrity and fast data transmission speeds of up to 2000Mbps. The full-metal shielding minimizes signal interference, maintaining high-quality, stable connections at all times.
RVers Special Tip:For scenarios requiring an Ethernet cable coupler or extender, choose Cat6A or better cables with 23AWG or higher and high-quality RJ45 connectors to ensure a reliable, stable connection. Given the potential instability of third-party PoE injectors, we recommend using regular Ethernet cables (23AWG or better) with a length of no more than 75ft for the most reliable performance.

Given the potential instability of third-party PoE injectors, we recommend using regular Ethernet cables (23AWG or better) with a length of no more than 75ft for the most reliable performance.
